The '''third person''' is one of the four [[grammatical persons]] in Aelaki. It refers to the topic or visible participant in the discourse.

== Ki Syllables ==

Third person [[Ki]] syllables vary by [[gender]]:

{| class="wikitable"
! Gender !! Ki Syllable !! Word-Final Form
|-
| Child || shu || -ush
|-
| Female || sho || -osh
|-
| Male || sha || -ash
|}

== Distinction from Fourth Person ==

The third person marks the '''proximate''' or topical participant — the one currently in focus in the discourse. This contrasts with the [[fourth person]] (obviative), which marks off-stage or backgrounded participants. The inanimate gender does not exist in third person; it is restricted to the [[fourth person]].

== Relationship to First Person ==

Certain [[first person]] noun types ('''1p→3p nouns''') are declined in the third person for jussive and optative constructions, bridging the two persons in specific grammatical contexts.

== Verb Agreement ==

In verb agreement, third person Ki markers appear in the following positions:

* '''Transitive verbs''': prefix Ki (subject) and suffix Ki (object)
* '''Intransitive active verbs''': prefix Ki (subject)
* '''Intransitive stative verbs''': suffix Ki (subject)

== See Also ==

* [[First person]]
* [[Second person]]
* [[Fourth person]]
* [[Ki]]

[[Category:grammatical persons]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
